content:
===BEGIN===
A black hole is a place in space where gravity is super strong—so strong that not even light can get away from it! Imagine if you had a super powerful vacuum cleaner that could suck up everything nearby. That’s kind of what a black hole does. It forms when a really big star runs out of fuel and collapses into a tiny, super heavy ball.

Because it’s so heavy and small, its gravity pulls everything close to it, even light! Light usually travels super fast and can escape most places, but near a black hole, the gravity is too strong. So, once something goes too close, it can’t escape and gets pulled in forever.

Scientists can’t see black holes directly because no light comes out, but they know they’re there because of how they affect nearby stars and gas. Black holes are like invisible space monsters with a super strong pull!
